1→# Lucky5 — Developer Guide
2→
3→Lucky5 is a clean-room recreation of a Lebanese amusement video poker machine (1990–2010 era). It features an ASP.NET Core 9 backend (REST API + SignalR hub) with a vanilla JS/CSS frontend, authentic Lebanese arcade aesthetics, four progressive jackpots, a Two Pair minimum qualifying hand, inline double-up Hi-Lo, and a deterministic architecture targeting 87.5% RTP.
4→
5→This document is the single source of truth for anyone working on the codebase. It covers architecture, game rules, key files, data flows, and operational details.
6→
7→---
8→
9→## Table of Contents
10→
11→1. [Repository Layout](#repository-layout)
12→2. [Architecture Overview](#architecture-overview)
13→3. [Backend Layers](#backend-layers)
14→4. [Frontend](#frontend)
15→5. [Game Rules & Mechanics](#game-rules--mechanics)
16→6. [Credit Accounting (Deferred Settlement)](#credit-accounting-deferred-settlement)
17→7. [Progressive Jackpots](#progressive-jackpots)
18→8. [Double-Up (Hi-Lo)](#double-up-hi-lo)
19→9. [RTP & Machine Policy](#rtp--machine-policy)
20→10. [API Reference](#api-reference)
21→11. [Authentication](#authentication)
22→12. [Configuration](#configuration)
23→13. [Running Locally](#running-locally)
24→14. [Deployment](#deployment)
25→15. [Reference Material](#reference-material)
26→
27→---
28→

38→│   │   │   ├── Middleware/          # Exception handling, bearer token extraction
39→│   │   │   ├── Program.cs          # App bootstrap, DI, middleware pipeline
40→│   │   │   └── wwwroot/            # Static frontend (HTML/CSS/JS/assets)
41→│   │   │       ├── index.html
42→│   │   │       ├── css/game.css

84→
85→---
86→
87→## Architecture Overview
88→
89→The system follows a three-layer deterministic engine design:
90→
91→```
92→┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
93→│  LAYER 3: Presentation Noise                        │
94→│  Frontend (vanilla JS) + server noise seed          │
95→│  Randomized timing, animations, visual effects      │
96→└─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
97→                          │ REST + SignalR
98→┌─────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────┐
99→│  LAYER 2: Machine Policy (MachinePolicy.cs)         │
100→│  RTP tracking, distribution modes, payout scaling   │
101→│  Deck composition alteration, streak management     │
102→└─────────────────────────┬───────────────────────────┘
103→                          │
104→┌─────────────────────────┴───────────────────────────┐
105→│  LAYER 1: Deterministic Core (CleanRoom/)           │
106→│  SplitMix64 RNG, 5-card draw, hand evaluation,     │
107→│  double-up engine, paytable, lineage profiles       │
108→└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
109→```
110→
111→All game outcomes are determined server-side. The frontend is a thin presentation layer that receives results via REST API calls and renders them with appropriate animations.
112→
113→---
114→
115→## Backend Layers
116→
117→### Lucky5.Domain — Core Engine (`Game/CleanRoom/`)
118→
119→This is the heart of the system. All files here are pure logic with no external dependencies.
120→
121→| File | Purpose |
122→|------|---------|
123→| `CoreModels.cs` | Enums and records: `HandCategory`, `PaytableProfile`, `RoundState`, card representations |
124→| `DeterministicRng.cs` | SplitMix64 seeded RNG — all randomness flows through this |
125→| `FiveCardDrawEngine.cs` | Deal 5 cards, evaluate holds, draw replacements, evaluate final hand |
126→| `Lucky5DoubleUpEngine.cs` | Hi-Lo double-up loop: dealer card, guess, switch, 5♠ switch-only trigger, SafeFail, MachineClosed |
127→| `MachinePolicy.cs` | RTP management: distribution modes (COLD/NEUTRAL/HOT), deck alteration, payout scaling |
128→| `LineageProfiles.cs` | Cabinet variant profiles (Golden Poker, Bonus Poker lineage) |
129→| `PresentationNoiseGenerator.cs` | Separate noise seed for timing/visual variance |
130→
131→### Lucky5.Domain — Entities
132→
133→| Entity | Purpose |
134→|--------|---------|
135→| `GameRound` | Single hand lifecycle: deal → draw → optional double-up → settle. Tracks `WinAmount`, `OriginalWinAmount`, `IsPayoutSettled` |
136→| `MachineLedgerState` | Per-machine state: wallet balance, RTP tracking (CapitalIn/Out), jackpot values, streak counters, distribution mode |
137→| `User` | Player identity and authentication |
138→| `Machine` | Machine configuration |
139→| `PokerCard` | Card representation (suit + rank) |
140→
141→### Lucky5.Application — Contracts & DTOs
142→
143→Service interfaces (`IGameService`, `IAuthService`) and all API data transfer objects. The DTOs define the exact shape of every API response.
144→
145→Key DTOs:
146→- `DealResultDto` — Cards dealt, round ID, jackpot state, advised holds
147→- `DrawResultDto` — Final hand, win amount, hand rank, updated jackpots
148→- `DoubleUpResultDto` — Guess result, current amount, wallet balance, status
149→
150→### Lucky5.Infrastructure — Service Implementations
151→
152→| Service | Purpose |
153→|---------|---------|
154→| `GameService.cs` | The main orchestrator — implements `IGameService`. Handles deal, draw, double-up, cashout, jackpot logic. ~1200 lines. |
155→| `InMemoryDataStore.cs` | Non-persistent storage for users, rounds, machines, ledger state. Replaced by database in production. |
156→| `AuthService.cs` | Login, signup, OTP verification |
157→| `SimpleTokenService.cs` | JWT token generation/validation |
158→
159→### Lucky5.Realtime — SignalR Hub
160→
161→`CarrePokerGameHub.cs` provides real-time communication for machine join/leave, state sync, and heartbeat. The game currently uses REST API for gameplay actions, with SignalR available for future real-time event fanout.
162→
163→---
164→
165→## Frontend
166→
167→The frontend is a single-page vanilla JS/CSS application served as static files from `wwwroot/`.
168→
169→### Key Files
170→
171→| File | Purpose |
172→|------|---------|
173→| `index.html` | Page structure, login/signup screens, game layout, button grid |
174→| `css/game.css` | All styling — retro arcade aesthetic, card designs, animations |
175→| `js/game.js` | All game logic — API calls, state machine, animations, UI updates |
176→
177→### Game States
178→
179→The frontend operates as a state machine with these states:
180→
181→| State | Description |
182→|-------|-------------|
183→| `idle` | Waiting for bet. BET button active. |
184→| `betting` | Bet placed, waiting for DEAL. BET and DEAL active. |
185→| `dealt` | Cards dealt, selecting holds. HOLD buttons + DEAL DRAW active. |
186→| `drawing` | Draw in progress (brief). |
187→| `result` | Hand evaluated, win/loss shown. If win → can enter double-up. |
188→| `win` | Win displayed, TAKE SCORE drains credits. |
189→| `doubleup` | In double-up mode. BIG/SMALL/SWITCH/TAKE SCORE/TAKE HALF active. |
190→| `shuffling` | Card shuffle animation during double-up. |
191→
192→### UI Layout (Top to Bottom)
193→
194→1. **Paytable** — Rainbow-colored hand rankings with live payout values
195→2. **Card Area** — 5 cards during play, idle FH selector card when not armed, jackpot siphon animation on jackpot wins, double-up history in the current live frontend
196→3. **Info Bar** — CREDIT | WIN | STAKE displays
197→4. **Jackpot Bar** — 4 progressive jackpot tickers (4K-A, FH, 4K-B, SF)
198→5. **Control Deck** — Physical-style beveled buttons on wood-grain background
199→
200→### Button Color Convention (Lebanese Cabinet)
201→
202→- **DEAL DRAW**: Red (opposite of Western convention)
203→- **BET**: Green (opposite of Western convention)
204→- **HOLD 1–5 / BIG / SMALL**: Amber/gold
205→- **TAKE SCORE**: Orange
206→- **TAKE HALF**: Red
207→- **CANCEL HOLD**: Cream/beige
208→
209→---
210→
211→## Game Rules & Mechanics
212→
213→### Basic Flow
214→
215→1. **Bet** — Player places bet (5,000–10,000, increment 100)
216→2. **Deal** — 5 cards dealt; the machine session must already hold enough credits for both deal and draw
217→3. **Hold/Draw** — Player selects holds (auto-hold suggests optimal); draw costs another bet
218→4. **Evaluate** — Hand ranked, payout calculated per Lebanese paytable
219→5. **Win/Lose** — If win, player can Take Score or enter Double-Up
220→6. **Double-Up** — Optional Hi-Lo game to multiply winnings
221→
222→### Paytable (Lebanese Profile)
223→
224→| Hand | Multiplier |
225→|------|-----------|
226→| Royal Flush | 1000x |
227→| Straight Flush | 75x |
228→| Four of a Kind | 15x |
229→| Full House | 12x |
230→| Flush | 10x |
231→| Straight | 8x |
232→| Three of a Kind | 3x |
233→| Two Pair | 2x |
234→
235→Minimum qualifying hand is **Two Pair**. Single pair does not pay.
236→
237→### Betting
238→
239→- Min: 5,000 / Max: 10,000 / Step: 100
240→- **Double deduction**: Deal costs 1x bet, Draw costs another 1x bet (total 2x per round)
241→- After completing a hand, first BET press resets to 5,000
242→- Starting credits: 200,000 (demo mode)
243→
244→### Auto-Hold
245→
246→After dealing, the engine suggests optimal holds:
247→- Priority: quads > full house > flush > straight > trips > two pair > 4-to-flush > 4-to-straight > pair
248→- Player can override by clicking individual cards
249→
250→---
251→
252→## Credit Accounting (Deferred Settlement)
253→
254→This is a critical architectural decision. Draw wins are **not** immediately credited to the player's wallet.
255→
256→### How It Works
257→
258→1. `DrawAsync` evaluates the hand and records `WinAmount` on the `GameRound` — but does **not** add it to the wallet balance
259→2. The WIN indicator displays the pending win separately from CREDITS
260→3. Settlement (wallet credit) only occurs at:
261→   - **Take Score** — Full cashout, triggers drain animation
262→   - **Take Half** — Instant partial cashout (half to wallet, half continues in double-up)
263→   - **FinalizeDoubleUp** — Double-up ends (win or lose)
264→
265→### Key Fields on GameRound
266→
267→| Field | Purpose |
268→|-------|---------|
269→| `WinAmount` | Current win value (may change via Take Half) |
270→| `OriginalWinAmount` | Immutable original draw win (for ledger delta tracking) |
271→| `IsPayoutSettled` | Idempotency guard — prevents double-crediting on repeated cashout calls |
272→
273→### Why Deferred Settlement?
274→
275→- Base paytable wins stay gamble-eligible
276→- Jackpot wins go straight to machine credits and never enter double-up
277→- `SettledAmount` prevents partial-cashout accounting bugs
278→- `IsPayoutSettled` prevents double-cashout exploits
279→
280→---
281→
282→## Progressive Jackpots
283→
284→Four jackpot slots are tracked per machine:
285→
286→| Slot | Trigger Condition | Max Value | Reset Value (20%) |
287→|------|------------------|-----------|-------------------|
288→| Full House (FH) | FH trips rank matches player's chosen rank | 25,000,000 | 5,000,000 |
289→| Four of a Kind A (4K-A) | 4K hit while slot A is active | 999,999 | 200,000 |
290→| Four of a Kind B (4K-B) | 4K hit while slot B is active | 999,999 | 200,000 |
291→| Straight Flush (SF) | Any straight flush | 20,000,000 | 4,000,000 |
292→
293→### Growth
294→
295→- **Flat +100** to every jackpot per hand played
296→- Both deal and draw phases contribute +100 each (so +200 total per completed hand)
297→- Machines can start at max jackpots
298→- On win, jackpot resets to 20% of max
299→- Jackpot values are capped and do not grow above max
300→
301→### 4K Slot Selection
302→
303→- Machine randomly selects which 4K slot (A or B) is active per round
304→- Only the active slot can be won; the active slot glows in the UI
305→- Selection is deterministic (based on game seed)
306→
307→### FH Jackpot Rank
308→
309→- The selected FH rank is shown as a center card while the game is idle
310→- After pressing BET for the next round, HOLD 1 can cycle that card/rank
311→- Only a Full House where the trips rank matches the selected rank triggers the jackpot
312→- Default rank: Ace
313→
314→### Royal Flush
315→
316→Royal Flush has no progressive jackpot but can be doubled up to the 50,000,000 credit limit.
317→
318→---
319→
320→## Double-Up (Hi-Lo)
321→
322→After any winning hand, the player can enter an inline Hi-Lo game to multiply their winnings.
323→
324→### Mechanics
325→
326→1. A **dealer card** is shown (face up)
327→2. Player guesses **BIG** (8–K) or **SMALL** (2–6)
328→3. A **player card** is drawn:
329→   - Correct guess: win amount doubles
330→   - Wrong guess: lose everything
331→   - **Ace**: always wins regardless of guess direction
332→   - **7**: always loses (push/lose)
333→
334→### Switch Mechanic
335→
336→- Player can **switch** the dealer card up to **2 times** per round
337→- Each switch consumes the next card from the deterministic deck
338→- Strategic: allows avoiding unfavorable dealer cards
339→
340→### Five of Spades (5♠) — Lucky Card
341→
342→When the **5 of Spades** appears as the dealer card (after a switch):
343→- Win amount is multiplied by **4x**
344→- **No-Lose mode** activates for that round
345→- If the player guesses wrong: **SafeFail** — the current amount is banked instead of lost
346→- Visual: yellow flash, "LUCKY 5" banner
347→
348→### MachineClosed
349→
350→If machine credits reach or pass **50,000,000** through the final double-up jump:
351→- the current double-up result cashes out automatically into machine credits
352→- the machine is marked as closed for that player session
353→- the machine ledger itself does not reset
354→- player can cash out machine credits back to the lobby wallet subject to the machine-session rules
355→
356→### Take Score vs Take Half
357→
358→| Action | Effect |
359→|--------|--------|
360→| Take Score | Ends double-up, full amount drains to credits with animation |
361→| Take Half | Instantly takes half to credits (no animation), continues double-up with remaining half |
362→
363→Take Half does **not** stop the double-up. If the remaining half is 0, it exits to idle.
364→
365→---
366→
367→## RTP & Machine Policy
368→
369→Target RTP: **87.5%** across all machines.
370→
371→### How RTP Is Achieved
372→
373→The natural base RTP from the paytable + double deduction is ~38%. The Machine Policy layer bridges this to 87.5% through two mechanisms:
374→
375→1. **Deck Composition Alteration** — Before dealing, the policy can add or remove cards:
376→   - **COLD mode**: Removes high cards and 5♠, making wins less likely
377→   - **NEUTRAL mode**: Standard deck
378→   - **HOT mode**: Injects extra 5♠ and high cards, making wins more likely
379→
380→2. **Payout Scaling** — Win payouts are multiplied by a scale factor (~2.3x base):
381→   - Small wins (2P, 3K): ~0.96x of the current base scale
382→   - Medium wins (ST, FL, FH): ~1.02x of the current base scale
383→   - Big wins (4K, SF, RF): ~1.08x of the current base scale
384→
385→The current live policy also subtracts observed **jackpot RTP** from the base-game scaling target. That prevents the old failure mode where jackpots pushed the real RTP far above target while the scale logic still behaved as if only paytable wins existed.
386→
387→### Streak Management
388→
389→The policy tracks player win/loss streaks to create natural-feeling rhythm:
390→- `ConsecutiveLosses` counter biases toward HOT mode after 5+ losses
391→- After 15+ consecutive losses: forced HOT mode with maximum deck alteration
392→- After a win: 2–4 round cooldown before allowing next HOT phase
393→- Tracks `RoundsSinceMediumWin` for medium-tier pacing
394→
395→### Final QA note
396→
397→- jackpot wins now feed directly into machine credits and never enter double-up
398→- machine close is only evaluated on a real collecting event (cashout / final winning jump), not merely because a switched Lucky 5 preview amount looks large on screen
399→- extra Lucky 5 pressure in double-up now leans more on **5♠** than on free ace-heavy decks, which keeps the feature visible without giving away as many automatic wins

532→### Key Design Decisions
533→
534→1. **Clean-room approach**: Game logic was independently reimplemented based on behavioral analysis, not code copying
535→2. **Deterministic engine**: All randomness flows through SplitMix64 with known seeds — every outcome is replayable
536→3. **Backend-authoritative**: Frontend is a thin display layer; all game state lives on the server
537→4. **Deferred settlement**: Wins are not credited until explicitly cashed out (prevents accounting bugs)
538→5. **Lebanese paytable**: Uses the specific multiplier profile from the original machines (Two Pair minimum, DEAL=red/BET=green convention)
539→
540→
541→## Machine Credit vs Wallet
542→
543→- Lobby balance is the player wallet.
544→- Game CREDIT is machine-session credit.
545→- Players cash in to the machine in **200,000** increments up to **1,000,000** total cash-in per session.
546→- Players can cash out machine credits back to the wallet when the machine is closed or when machine credits reach at least **2x** the session cash-in amount.
547→- Machine closed is only a gameplay event. It does **not** reset the machine ledger or RTP memory.
548→
549→## Variance Smoothing / Fun Policy
550→
551→The policy layer now also reacts to long Lucky 5 droughts, net-since-close pressure, and strongly counterintuitive player behavior. This lets the online version soften very long dry spells while still using the target RTP and machine-ledger driven policy loop. The intention is **not** to force unavoidable jackpots; counterplay pressure is only a soft variance smoother so players cannot reliably exploit strange play into guaranteed jackpot outcomes. Lucky 5 itself is now intentionally stricter: the no-lose mode only arms when 5♠ is reached through SWITCH, never from the opening dealer card and never from a revealed BIG/SMALL result card.
552→
553→## Admin Tooling
554→
555→Admin accounts can: 
556→
557→- credit or debit player wallets without resetting machines
558→- inspect machine RTP / net-since-close / streak / jackpot state
559→- inspect per-player machine sessions and current machine credits
560→- reset machine state separately when needed
561→
562→
563→## Handoff
564→
565→See **[CONTINUATION_GUIDE.md](CONTINUATION_GUIDE.md)** for the fastest catch-up path for the next developer.

1→# Continuation Guide
2→
3→This document is the fastest way for another developer to catch up on the current Lucky5 codebase.
4→
5→## Live frontend
6→
7→The production frontend is the vanilla app served from:
8→
9→- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/index.html`
10→- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/js/game.js`
11→- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/wwwroot/css/game.css`
12→
13→Do **not** start from the prototype folders for feature work unless you are intentionally rebuilding the frontend.
14→
15→## Core backend files
16→
17→- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/Controllers/GameController.cs` — gameplay, machine session cash-in/out, jackpot rank, machine state
18→- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/Controllers/AuthController.cs` — signup/login/OTP/profile/history
19→- `server/src/Lucky5.Api/Controllers/AdminController.cs` — admin-only user/machine tools
20→- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/GameService.cs` — authoritative game flow
21→- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/AdminService.cs` — admin balance adjustments and machine inspection/reset
22→- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/AuthService.cs` — auth and wallet profile operations
23→- `server/src/Lucky5.Infrastructure/Services/InMemoryDataStore.cs` — all in-memory state
24→- `server/src/Lucky5.Domain/Game/CleanRoom/MachinePolicy.cs` — RTP / variance shaping / double-up deck pressure
25→
26→## Economy model
27→
28→There are now two balances:
29→
30→1. **Lobby wallet balance**
31→   - shown in the lobby / wallet screens
32→   - stored on `MemberProfile.WalletBalance`
33→2. **Machine credit balance**
34→   - shown on the game CREDIT display
35→   - stored per player + machine in `MachineSessionState.MachineCredits`
36→
37→### Machine session rules
38→
39→- players cash in to the machine in **200,000** increments
40→- total cash-in per machine session is capped at **1,000,000**
41→- cash-out to wallet is allowed when either:
42→  - the machine session is marked closed, or
43→  - machine credits reach at least **2x** total cash-in
44→
45→## Jackpot / double-up rules
46→
47→- jackpot values can start at max
48→- jackpot wins reset to 20% baseline values
49→- jackpot values never grow above their max caps
50→- jackpot win value goes straight to **machine credits**
51→- only the **base paytable win** enters double-up
52→- the Lucky 5 no-lose state only arms when 5♠ is reached through **SWITCH**; opening dealer 5♠ and BIG/SMALL reveal 5♠ are visual only
53→- the paytable row for the active hand is reused as the live score row during win/double-up
54→
55→## Machine closed
56→
57→Machine closed is a gameplay event, not a hard reset.
58→
59→- machine ledgers continue accumulating history
60→- machine session is flagged closed for the current player
61→- player is expected to cash out machine credits back to the wallet
62→- machine RTP memory should keep running across closes
63→
64→## RTP / fun policy
65→
66→`MachinePolicy.cs` now uses:
67→
68→- target RTP
69→- jackpot RTP pressure (not just base paytable RTP)
70→- net since last close
71→- Lucky 5 drought
72→- loss streak pressure
73→- counterplay / counterintuitive-hold pressure
74→
75→This is meant to soften very long online dry spells while still letting the machine breathe with ups/downs and close misses.
76→
77→The latest tuning pass also tightened the negative correction when the observed RTP is already above target, so jackpot-heavy periods push the normal paytable scale down faster instead of compounding the overshoot.
78→
79→## Strange player behavior handling
80→
81→`GameService.DrawAsync()` compares actual holds against advised holds and updates `MachineSessionState.CounterplayScore`.
82→
83→This is then fed back into:
84→
85→- deal-time policy mode selection
86→- draw-time payout scale pressure
87→
88→So if a player repeatedly destroys strong hands or plays in an obviously counterintuitive way, the machine can compensate and keep the experience from becoming dead or overly punishing.
89→
90→## Admin tools
91→
92→Current admin features:
93→
94→- search/list users
95→- credit or debit wallet balances with reasons
96→- inspect machine RTP and jackpot state
97→- inspect per-player machine sessions on each machine
98→- reset machine state separately from wallet adjustments
99→
100→## Adding a new lobby game
101→
102→1. Add a new entry to `AVAILABLE_GAMES` in `wwwroot/js/game.js`
103→2. Add any new route/screen handling in `showLobby()` / `openGame()`
104→3. Add the new backend controller/service if the game needs its own logic
105→4. Keep the existing Lucky5 frontend isolated so variants do not break the live cabinet
106→
107→## Known limitation / next important step
108→
109→Machine ledgers and machine sessions still live in memory. That means they can survive for long-running single-instance deployments, but **not** across process restarts or redeploys.
110→
111→Also note that `DealAsync()` now requires enough machine credits for **both** deal and draw up front. That is deliberate: it avoids dead-end rounds where a player can start a hand but cannot afford the mandatory draw cost.
112→
113→For true month-scale durability, the next big engineering step is persistent storage for:
114→
115→- `MachineLedgerState`
116→- `MachineSessionState`
117→- optionally audit/history rows
118→

content.text: I’m mapping the remaining docs and repo structure now, then I’ll synthesize that with the backend/frontend branch reports into a vNext plan artifact.[TOOL {"calls":["ls .windsurf","ls .windsurf/workflows","read docs/FINAL_QA_AND_RTP_PASS_2026-03-13.md[1-160]","read docs/RTP_REBALANCING_ARCHITECTURE.md[1-220]","read docs/REBALANCING_SUMMARY_FINAL.md[1-160]","read docs/REBALANCING_RESULTS_85_RTP.md[1-160]","read docs/REBALANCING_PLAN_85_RTP.md[1-220]","read docs/ANDROID_BUILD.md[1-120]","read docs/CLOUD_RUN_DEPLOYMENT.md[1-120]","read docs/forensics/endpoint_catalog.md[1-160]","read docs/forensics/gameplay_event_catalog.md[1-140]","read docs/forensics/module_map.md[1-160]","read AZURE_DEPLOYMENT_GUIDE.md[1-140]","read AZURE_MULTI_REGION_GUIDE.md[1-120]","read Arcade Game RNG Simulation Model.md[1-220]"]}]

1) Current screen / menu structure
Vanilla cabinet flow (
index.html)
Primary screens are stacked in one page and toggled with .active / display:

Auth screen: login/signup bootstrap
index.html:23-83
Lobby screen: machine picker + wallet summary + bottom nav
index.html:85-131
Wallet screen: read-only balance/history view
index.html:133-154
Admin screen: player search + machine/RTP management
index.html:156-176
Game screen: core cabinet gameplay
index.html:178-293
Overlay game menu: cash in / cash out / back to lobby / reset / logout
index.html:296-303
Navigation wiring (
game.js)
Main screen transitions are direct DOM toggles:

showLobby() → game.js:1781-1794
showWallet() → game.js:1796-1806
showAdmin() → game.js:1883-1895
openGame() → game.js:1763-1779
backToLobbyFromGame() → game.js:2004-2023
event binding bootstraps on DOM ready → game.js:2086-2310
Styling / layout
Lobby nav + wallet/admin full-screen patterns are defined in CSS, not componentized:
lobby nav: game.css:1645-1679
wallet screen: game.css:1681-1837
admin screen: game.css:1958-2018
React src/web status
The React cabinet is useful as a play surface / telemetry prototype, but it does not currently mirror the vanilla app’s lobby/wallet/admin/menu UX:

single cabinet shell with machine buttons + diagnostics + wallet trail
no equivalent lobby nav, overlay menu, or admin flow
see src/web/components/lucky5-cabinet.tsx:131-669
So for navigation/menu/admin/player UX, the vanilla wwwroot app is still the real source of truth.

2) Player / admin menu gaps
Player gaps
“Back to Lobby” is hidden inside the in-game menu

menu button + menu overlay: index.html:287-303
handler: game.js:2248-2250
This is discoverable only after opening the menu; there is no persistent visible back affordance in the game itself.
Wallet is lobby-only

lobby wallet CTA: 
index.html:111
wallet nav item only exists on lobby bottom bar: index.html:122-125
Once in game, wallet access is indirect via menu cash-out, not via a consistent wallet/status surface.
Menu availability is state-gated

menu opens only in idle, win, or doubleup: game.js:2190-2193
During hold, the player cannot open the menu.
That may be intentional, but it increases stuck-feeling risk during long sessions.
Machine-closed UX depends on menu wording

repeated messages instruct user to use menu for cash out: game.js:221-225, 964, 1250, 1443
If player doesn’t realize menu is the only route, recovery feels opaque.
Admin gaps
Admin is only exposed from lobby bottom nav

hidden/shown by role: index.html:126-129, game.js:1707-1710
No persistent admin shortcut once inside wallet/game.
Admin actions are prompt/confirm driven

wallet credit/debit uses prompt(): game.js:1932-1951
machine reset uses confirm(): game.js:1990-1998, 2229-2238
Functional, but brittle and low-trust for operational use.
Two reset entry points

in-game menu reset: 
index.html:301, game.js:2229-2238
admin machine-list reset: game.js:1985-1998
This duplicates mental models and risks inconsistent future behavior.
3) Button / navigation reliability risks
Highest-risk patterns
A. Manual screen toggling is duplicated and easy to drift
Different functions individually remove/add classes across screens:

openGame() → game.js:1763-1779
showLobby() → 1781-1794
showWallet() → 1796-1806
showAdmin() → 1883-1895
logout reset path → 1642-1656
warningRisk:warning one future screen/state change can break another path because there’s no single authoritative router/state machine.

B. Menu UI is inline-styled in HTML
index.html:296-303
warningRisk:warning hard to maintain, hard to relocate “Back to Lobby” into a better structure, easy to visually drift from 
game.css.

C. Game re-entry is sticky via sessionStorage
machine persisted: 
game.js:1768, 2053, 2295-2300
warningRisk:warning on refresh, user is auto-dropped back into the game if lucky5_machineId exists, which can mask broken exit flows and create “why am I trapped in machine view?” complaints.

D. showLobby() is async but often called like sync
async definition: 
game.js:1781
called without await from backToLobbyFromGame(): 2022
also called from login bootstrap / restore paths
This probably works, but it’s a sequencing smell because lobby rendering depends on loadAvailableMachines().

E. Nav affordances are inconsistent across contexts
lobby has bottom nav: index.html:117-130
wallet/admin only have back buttons: 
index.html:135, 158
game has only menu overlay path: index.html:296-303
warningRisk:warning too many different navigation metaphors in one app.

F. Admin visibility is client-side UX only
hide/show admin/reset by role: game.js:1707-1710
That’s fine for UX, but from a reliability perspective it means the UI layer is doing conditional exposure in multiple places instead of one centralized capability map.

4) vNext priorities
P0 — unify navigation model
Create a single screen router/helper for:

auth
lobby
wallet
admin
game
menu open/close
Refactor targets:

game.js:1763-1806
game.js:1883-1895
game.js:2004-2023
game.js:2086-2310
Why: this is the root fix for button reliability and future menu relocation.

P1 — move “Back to Lobby” out of the overlay-only pattern
Current:

button lives in menu: 
index.html:300
wired at game.js:2248-2250
Recommendation:

put a persistent cabinet-safe back/exit affordance in the game chrome
keep menu for cash in/out + logout
if user request is “move go-back-to-lobby into existing menu,” then at minimum restyle/reframe it as the primary exit item and make its availability clearer when machine is closed
Why: this is the most important player-navigation pain point.

P1 — centralize machine-close recovery UX
Current machine-closed messaging is scattered:

game.js:221-225
964
1244-1250
1437-1443
Recommendation:

one helper for closed-machine state
one consistent CTA path
ensure menu/back/cash-out affordances remain reachable in every relevant post-close state
Why: directly tied to the reported invalid/close/reset/cashout confusion.

P2 — replace admin prompt()/confirm() flows with inline panel controls
Targets:

game.js:1932-1951
game.js:1990-1998
game.js:2229-2238
Why: improves trust, prevents malformed input, and makes admin actions auditable/clear.

P2 — bring menu styling into CSS and de-duplicate UI logic
Move menu panel/button styles from:

index.html:296-303
Into:

game.css near wallet/admin/lobby navigation sections (1645-2018 is the right neighborhood)
Why: needed before reshaping the menu without making HTML even more brittle.

P3 — decide whether React cabinet is replacing or only shadowing vanilla
React current scope:

machine selection / deal-draw / double-up / diagnostics
src/web/components/lucky5-cabinet.tsx:256-379, 408-669
Missing vs vanilla:

lobby/wallet/admin/menu routing
session resume/back-to-lobby/menu semantics
Why: right now there are effectively two frontends, but only one complete UX. That increases future drift.

Bottom line
The vanilla wwwroot frontend already has the right broad structure, but navigation is held together by duplicated DOM toggles, an inline overlay menu, and inconsistent exit patterns. The biggest UX reliability issue is not a single broken button—it’s that screen transitions, machine recovery, and exit behavior are spread across multiple ad hoc handlers instead of one navigation model.

If you want, I can turn this into a tighter handoff artifact with:

exact bug hypotheses
recommended code touch list
verification checklist for all lobby/game/admin buttons.

1→# Final QA and RTP Pass — 2026-03-13
2→
3→This document records the last debugging / optimization pass applied to the current repo snapshot.
4→
5→## What was fixed in this pass
6→
7→### 1. Deal / draw affordability bug
8→
9→The live frontend and backend could previously let a player start a round with only enough machine credits for the **deal** even though the game always requires a second bet for the **draw**.
10→
11→Fix:
12→
13→- `GameService.DealAsync()` now requires enough credits for **deal + draw** up front
14→- `wwwroot/js/game.js` now blocks starting the hand unless the player has at least `currentBet * 2`
15→
16→Result:
17→
18→- no more dead-end hands where the player can deal but cannot legally draw
19→
20→### 2. Lucky 5 machine-close timing bug
21→
22→The older double-up logic could close the machine too early when a `5S` switch inflated the preview amount, even before a real winning guess had happened.
23→
24→Fix:
25→
26→- `Lucky5DoubleUpEngine` no longer auto-closes on session start or on switch preview
27→- machine close is now evaluated only on the **actual winning collection step**
28→- added regression coverage for the "5S switch near cap" path
29→
30→Result:
31→
32→- `5S` can still create the dramatic preview multiplier
33→- the machine only closes when the real post-win amount would actually push collected credits over the cap
34→
35→### 3. Async blocking cleanup
36→
37→`GameService` still had `.Result` calls inside async gameplay paths.
38→
39→Fix:
40→
41→- `DoubleUpAsync()` and `GuessDoubleUpAsync()` were cleaned up to use proper `await`
42→
43→Result:
44→
45→- lower deadlock / blocking risk in the request path
46→
47→### 4. Shared ledger locking
48→
49→Machine/admin/game code paths were not using the same lock object.
50→
51→Fix:
52→
53→- added `InMemoryDataStore.LedgerSync`
54→- switched gameplay/admin machine-ledger mutations to the shared lock
55→
56→Result:
57→
58→- lower risk of inconsistent machine-state mutation under concurrent access
59→
60→### 5. Jackpot-aware payout scaling
61→
62→The biggest RTP bug found during the pass was that the payout-scale logic was mostly looking at **base-game RTP** and only weakly correcting for jackpot overshoot.
63→
64→Fix:
65→
66→- `MachinePolicyState` now exposes `JackpotRtp`
67→- `MachinePolicy.ResolvePayoutScale()` now reduces the **base-game target** by observed jackpot RTP before deciding the scale
68→- overshoot correction was made materially stronger when live RTP is already above target
69→- fun-pressure bonuses are now damped when the machine is already overshooting
70→- win-tier spread was tightened from a wider small/medium/big separation to a narrower one
71→
72→Result:
73→
74→- jackpot-heavy periods pull the ordinary paytable scale down faster
75→- the machine still keeps rhythm / suspense, but it does not compound overshoot as aggressively
76→
77→### 6. Double-up pressure tuning
78→
79→The double-up deck pressure previously leaned too much on extra aces in hot states, which is especially generous because aces are auto-win cards in the current rule set.
80→
81→Fix:
82→
83→- reduced extra ace injection in `MachinePolicy.BuildDoubleUpDeck()`
84→- left Lucky 5 pressure focused more on `5S` than on free ace-heavy decks
85→
86→Result:
87→
88→- Lucky 5 remains visible and dramatic
89→- neutral / hot double-up becomes less automatically player-favorable than before
90→
91→### 7. Frontend machine-close guard after jackpot fill
92→
93→A jackpot animation could finish and immediately auto-enter double-up even when the machine was already effectively closed.
94→
95→Fix:
96→
97→- `game.js` now stops the auto-start and shows `MACHINE CLOSED - TAKE SCORE / CASH OUT` if jackpot fill reaches the machine-credit limit first
98→
99→## Replay / regression updates
100→
101→- replay test setup now cashes into the machine session before dealing
102→- added coverage for the Lucky 5 switch + machine close timing path
103→
104→## Approximate RTP simulation notes used during tuning
105→
106→A lightweight mirror simulation was used during this pass to check **behavioral direction**. It is useful for tuning, but it is still an approximation of the live service path rather than a formal authoritative production certification run.
107→
108→The main findings from that tuning pass were:
109→
110→- raw base-game RTP versus the full **deal + draw** cost was roughly **0.455**
111→- base game plus old payout scaling without jackpots sat around **0.883**
112→- once jackpot overlay was included, the older scale logic could overshoot badly because jackpot RTP was not being compensated enough
113→- after switching to jackpot-aware payout scaling, the mirrored total moved much closer to the target band (roughly around **0.90** in the tuning pass instead of the previous catastrophic overshoot)
114→- the biggest remaining generosity comes from **double-up**, especially with optimal or near-optimal player behavior
115→
116→## Important remaining limitation
117→
118→This pass improved the policy loop and removed several correctness bugs, but it does **not** claim that the current in-memory build has been mathematically certified to 87.5% across every long-run player strategy.
119→
120→The remaining high-variance area is still the double-up layer:
121→
122→- Ace-as-auto-win is generous
123→- optimal BIG/SMALL play remains strong
124→- true month-scale balancing should eventually be measured from a refreshed simulation harness that matches the live service rules exactly
125→
126→## Recommended next step if more tuning is needed
127→
128→If you want one more balancing pass after this, focus on only one thing next:
129→
130→1. refresh the simulation harness so it mirrors the live service exactly
131→2. then tune double-up EV using data rather than intuition
132→
133→That is the cleanest next lever if the production telemetry still trends hot.
134→
EOF

1→# Cloud Run Deployment
2→
3→This is the recommended easiest deployment path for Lucky5.
4→
5→## Why this is the easiest option
6→
7→- frontend and backend stay in one ASP.NET Core app
8→- no separate static hosting step is required
9→- WebSockets / SignalR work on Cloud Run
10→- the deploy script pins the app to **min 1 / max 1** to preserve in-memory machine history and RTP memory as much as possible
11→- the script auto-enables the required Google APIs and auto-detects the final Cloud Run URL
12→
13→## Before the first deploy
14→
15→1. Install the Google Cloud SDK.
16→2. Run `gcloud auth login`.
17→3. Create or choose a Google Cloud project with billing enabled.
18→4. Pick a region (the script defaults to `me-west1`).
19→
20→## One-command deploy
21→
22→The script can now prompt for missing values interactively, so the shortest path is simply:
23→
24→```bash
25→bash scripts/deploy-cloud-run.sh
26→```
27→
28→If you prefer explicit environment variables, you can still run:
29→
30→```bash
31→PROJECT_ID=your-project \
32→REGION=me-west1 \
33→SERVICE_NAME=lucky5 \
34→LUCKY5_ADMIN_USERNAME=admin \
35→LUCKY5_ADMIN_PASSWORD=change-me-now \
36→bash scripts/deploy-cloud-run.sh
37→```
38→
39→If you want to allow an extra custom domain or a separate frontend origin later, add it as a comma-separated list:
40→
41→```bash
42→PROJECT_ID=your-project \
43→ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://your-custom-domain.com,https://your-run-url.run.app \
44→bash scripts/deploy-cloud-run.sh
45→```
46→
47→## What the script does
48→
49→- enables `run.googleapis.com`, `cloudbuild.googleapis.com`, and `artifactregistry.googleapis.com`
50→- deploys the repo directly to Cloud Run from the root `Dockerfile`
51→- sets the app to `min 1 / max 1` with session affinity
52→- reads the final service URL and uses it as the default allowed origin when you do not provide one
53→- prompts for project / region / service / admin credentials when you do not pre-set them in the shell
54→
55→## Important note about machine history
56→
57→The machine ledger does **not** reset when a machine closes. Machine closed is only a gameplay event. However, the repo still stores machine history in memory, so a full process restart or redeploy can still clear history. For truly month-scale durability, the next step is persistent storage for machine ledgers and machine sessions.
58→
59→## Optional next improvements
60→
61→- move machine ledgers and sessions into Cloud SQL or Firestore for durable history
62→- add a custom domain on Cloud Run
63→- store admin credentials in Secret Manager instead of plain environment variables
64→
EOF
